mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a component of the CPU?
mcqs.explanation: Main Memory (RAM) is external to the CPU and is part of the memory subsystem. The CPU consists of Control Unit, ALU, and Registers.

mcqs.question: Which register holds the address of the next instruction to be executed?
mcqs.explanation: The Program Counter (PC) contains the address of the next instruction to be fetched from memory.

mcqs.question: The stored-program concept was proposed by:
mcqs.explanation: John von Neumann proposed the stored-program concept where both instructions and data are stored in the same memory.

mcqs.question: Which bus carries memory addresses from CPU to memory?
mcqs.explanation: The Address Bus carries address information from CPU to memory to specify which memory location to access.

mcqs.question: What does ALU stand for?
mcqs.explanation: ALU stands for Arithmetic Logic Unit, which performs arithmetic and logical operations in the CPU.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of Primary Memory?
mcqs.explanation: Primary memory (RAM) is volatile, fast, and directly accessed by the CPU for temporary data storage.

mcqs.question: The Fetch-Decode-Execute cycle is performed by which component?
mcqs.explanation: The Control Unit manages the Fetch-Decode-Execute cycle by generating appropriate control signals.

mcqs.question: Which register temporarily holds data being transferred between CPU and memory?
mcqs.explanation: MDR (Memory Data Register) acts as a buffer to hold data during transfer between CPU and memory.

mcqs.question: What is the word length of a 64-bit processor?

options.text: The CPU can process 8 bits at a time
The CPU can process 16 bits at a time
The CPU can process 64 bits at a time
The CPU can process 32 bits at a time

mcqs.explanation: Word length refers to the number of bits the CPU processes in a single operation, so a 64-bit processor processes 64 bits at a time.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT part of the instruction cycle?
mcqs.explanation: Compile is a pre-execution phase done by software. The instruction cycle consists of Fetch, Decode, Execute, and Store phases.

mcqs.question: What type of memory loses its contents when power is turned off?
mcqs.explanation: RAM (Random Access Memory) is volatile memory that loses its contents when power is disconnected.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an example of Secondary Memory?
mcqs.explanation: Hard Disk Drive (HDD) is secondary storage - non-volatile and used for permanent data storage.

mcqs.question: The Control Unit and ALU together form which unit?
mcqs.explanation: The Processing Unit consists of the Control Unit and ALU, which together perform all data processing operations.

mcqs.question: In a microprogrammed control unit, what controls the execution of microinstructions?
mcqs.explanation: In microprogrammed control, microinstructions are stored in control store (ROM/PROM) and sequence through a microsequencer.

mcqs.question: Which register holds the instruction that is currently being executed?
mcqs.explanation: The Instruction Register (IR) holds the instruction that has been fetched from memory and is currently being decoded and executed.

mcqs.question: What is the main function of the system bus?

options.text: To connect all major components of the computer

mcqs.explanation: The system bus connects the CPU, memory, and I/O devices, providing pathways for data, address, and control signals.

mcqs.question: The number of bits that can be transferred at once is determined by:
mcqs.explanation: The data bus width determines how many bits can be transferred simultaneously between components.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of RISC architecture?
mcqs.explanation: RISC (Reduced Instruction Set Computer) typically has a large register set to reduce memory access and simplify instruction execution.

mcqs.question: What is the clock cycle time measured in?
mcqs.explanation: Clock cycle time is measured in Hertz (Hz), representing the number of clock cycles per second.

mcqs.question: What is the function of the Status Register (Flag register)?

options.text: To store condition flags after arithmetic/logic operations

mcqs.explanation: The Status Register (or Flag register) stores condition codes/flags like Zero, Carry, Overflow, Sign that result from operations.
